GHSA-g8p2-7wf7-98mq.title: 1-Click RCE via Authentication Token Exfiltration from gatewayUrl
GHSA-g8p2-7wf7-98mq.description: OpenClaw UI auto-trusts gateway URLs and opens an authenticated WebSocket with no user interaction required. Authentication tokens are handed over to an attacker-controlled gateway, enabling 1-click RCE.
GHSA-g8p2-7wf7-98mq.coverage_note: T68 INSECURE_DEFAULT_CONFIG fires when allow_remote=true appears without authentication. The gatewayUrl auto-trust is the default config failure T68 was built to catch. AuditScanner Phase 3 flags this as CRITICAL.

GHSA-q284-4pvr-m585.title: OS Command Injection via Project Root Path in sshNodeCommand
GHSA-q284-4pvr-m585.description: Path traversal in the SSH command execution path leads to arbitrary command injection. Reported by Armin Ronacher (mitsuhiko), creator of Flask and Jinja2.
GHSA-q284-4pvr-m585.coverage_note: T03 SHELL_INJECTION catches the command injection class. T04 PATH_TRAVERSAL catches the traversal entry vector. T70 ENV_PATH_HIJACK covers the PATH manipulation angle. Together they cover the full attack chain documented in this advisory.

GHSA-mc68-q9jw-2h3v.title: Command Injection in Clawdbot Docker Execution via PATH Environment Variable
GHSA-mc68-q9jw-2h3v.description: Manipulated PATH environment variable in Docker execution context redirects execution to an attacker-controlled binary. No shell metacharacters required -- invisible to standard shell injection detection.
GHSA-mc68-q9jw-2h3v.coverage_note: T70 ENV_PATH_HIJACK was built specifically in response to this advisory. Fires on PATH, LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, NODE_PATH modification in execution contexts -- the exact attack class GHSA-mc68-q9jw-2h3v documents. This is the gap T03 SHELL_INJECTION could not close because there are no metacharacters in this attack pattern.
